topic.what: Adversarial malware generator for Linux ELF binaries using semantic-preserving transformations
topic.how: Genetic algorithm workflow with 12 modification types and 7 data sources to generate evasive samples while preserving functionality
topic.why_matters: Limited work on ELF adversarial malware despite Linux growth in HPC/cloud/IoT; addresses interpretability of ML-based detection through advanced logging

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of malware samples misclassified as benign by MalConv classifier
characteristics.vs_baseline: baseline: original malware correctly detected by classifier
characteristics.evidence: Table 3 comparing all generators; 67.74% achieved with Generator 1 using all modifications and data sources
characteristics.context: 100 test malware samples submitted after discarding pre-existing misclassifications

characteristics.what_it_measures: Mean change in malware label confidence between original and adversarial samples
characteristics.vs_baseline: negative indicates reduced confidence in malware classification
characteristics.evidence: Equation 6 in Section 3; MD metric computed across all test samples
characteristics.context: Mean Difference in Confidence metric proposed alongside Extended Evasion Rate

characteristics.what_it_measures: MalConv detection accuracy on validation dataset
characteristics.vs_baseline: trained from scratch on Labeled-Elfs dataset
characteristics.evidence: Section 5: trained for 5 epochs with cross-entropy loss and Adam optimizer

characteristics.what_it_measures: Percentage of test malware correctly detected before adversarial modifications
characteristics.evidence: Section 6: test set consists of 160 malware files only
characteristics.context: Baseline detection rate; samples already misclassified are excluded from ER calculation

characteristics.what_itasures: Number of distinct transformation types available in generator
characteristics.vs_baseline: compared to PE adversarial generators (10-16 types)
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.1 lists all 12 modification types including Add Section, Rotate Segments, Change Instructions
characteristics.context: Enables diverse mutation space for genetic algorithm

characteristics.what_it_measures: Number of distinct data source types for adversarial content injection
characteristics.vs_baseline: more data sources than typical PE generators
characteristics.evidence: Section 4.2: Binary Zeros, Random, Static File, Extracted Strings, Extracted Symbols (Dynamic/Static), Benign Executable

good_for: research on classifier sensitivity to string content
understanding modification space for Linux binaries

novelty: First comprehensive ELF adversarial malware generator
Extended Evasion Rate metric for confidence distribution analysis
12 modification types and 7 data sources for diverse transformations
Ablation study comparing modification type groups and data source groups
open_problems: Achieving higher evasion rates within reasonable computation time
Extending to ARM architecture for IoT applications
Deeper execution testing and dynamic analysis
Developing defensive strategies to strengthen classifiers
Validating findings on larger datasets typical of PE malware research
